description: A modern abstract or neo-icon painting of the Baptism of Christ by the Polish artist Jerzy Nowosielski. The figures are rendered in a simplified, geometric iconographic style with bold colors and flat planes, blending Byzantine icon tradition with 20th-century abstraction.
theological_description: The Baptism of Christ (MAT.3.13-17) inaugurates Jesus's public ministry. John baptizes Jesus in the Jordan, the heavens open, the Spirit descends like a dove, and the Father's voice declares: 'This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased' (MAT.3.17). Nowosielski's abstract treatment emphasizes the timeless, sacramental dimension of the event — every baptism participates in Christ's baptism.

scripture_refs.reason: Baptized into Christ's death and raised to new life
